walkthrough.title: Campaign Setup (First Game Only)
walkthrough.text: Before your very first game, locate the Victory and Defeat card decks. Place the Gold Victory Card facedown, then shuffle the 6 Blue Victory Cards and stack them on top. Do the same for the Defeat deck: Gold on the bottom, 6 Black Defeat Cards shuffled on top. You only need to do this once for the whole campaign.

walkthrough.title: Place the Board
walkthrough.text: Set the main board in front of you. The board shows a river running across the top half with outlined spaces for Canal Cards, a settlement area in the bottom-right with spaces for Hut Cards, and a card row along the top edge for Townsfolk and Barbarian Cards.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Townsfolk Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 30 Townsfolk Cards. Place 10 facedown below the board in the second space from the left to form your Ready Pile. The space to its left is for your Exhausted Pile. Place the remaining Townsfolk Cards facedown to the left of the board as the Townsfolk Draw Pile, leaving space for a Discard Pile. Then reveal 1 Townsfolk Card into each space above the board except the rightmost space.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Barbarian Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 16 Barbarian Cards and place them facedown to the right of the board as the Barbarian Draw Pile, leaving space for a Discard Pile. Reveal 1 Barbarian Card and place it faceup in the rightmost space above the board. You should now see a row of Townsfolk Cards with one Barbarian Card on the far right.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Canal Cards
walkthrough.text: Separate the 10 Canal Cards into 3 piles by the jade numbers on their backs. Shuffle each pile separately. Place 1 card from the '1-2 Jade' pile faceup on each of the 2 leftmost river spaces, 1 card from '3-4 Jade' on each of the 2 middle spaces, and 1 card from '5-6 Jade' on the 2 rightmost spaces. Return unused Canal Cards to the box.

walkthrough.title: Place Hut Cards and Buildings
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 6 Hut Cards and place 1 facedown on each of the 4 outlined spaces in the settlement area. Return the extras to the box unseen. Then place the 3 Farms, 4 Outposts, and 4 Huts in their starting positions as shown on the board.

walkthrough.title: Place the Flood and Barge
walkthrough.text: Place the Flood token at the far-left end of the river. Place the Barge on top of the leftmost Canal Card. The Flood will advance each time your Ready Pile runs out. The Barge marks which Canal section you can build next.

walkthrough.title: Create the Main Supply
walkthrough.text: Place all Workers (10 White, 6 each of Red, Blue, Black, and Yellow), Cowrie Shells, Clay, Wood, and Provisions into a shared Main Supply nearby. Keep the Story Book and Story Deck close but do not look through them. The back of the rulebook serves as a player aid.

walkthrough.title: You Are Ready
walkthrough.text: Your board should now show 6 Canal Cards along the river, a row of Townsfolk and Barbarian Cards above, 4 facedown Hut Cards in the settlement, and all buildings in their starting positions. You begin with 1 build site, 1 Hut space, 1 tuck space, and 1 trade available. More will unlock as you build Canals and construct buildings. Time to start Round 1!

walkthrough.title: Round Overview
walkthrough.text: Each round follows 5 phases in order: 1) Harvest, 2) Take Actions, 3) Return Barge, 4) Suffer Attacks, 5) Refresh Card Row. You repeat rounds until you either win by completing all 6 Canal sections or lose through one of three loss conditions. Let's walk through each phase.

walkthrough.title: Phase 1: Harvest
walkthrough.text: At the start of each round, gain all items shown in the Harvest row. In your first round, you typically draw 4 Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile into your hand, gain 1 Labourer (White Worker), 1 Cowrie Shell, and 1 Provision. You also gain 1 Cowrie Shell per constructed Outpost. Return any Workers on Hut spaces to your supply. As the game progresses, constructed Farms and tucked Townsfolk Cards will add more to your Harvest.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Take Actions — Playing Townsfolk Cards
walkthrough.text: During the action phase, you must use all Townsfolk Cards in your hand. Each card can be used in one of three ways: Play it to your Exhausted Pile to gain the items on the brown background. Discard it to the Townsfolk Discard Pile to gain items from both the brown AND red backgrounds (more resources, but you lose the card permanently). Or tuck it beneath the board to add its harvest icons to every future round. You cannot hold cards between rounds.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Building the Canal
walkthrough.text: Once per round, you may build the next Canal section if the Barge is on the leftmost Canal Card. Spend the Workers and Cowrie Shells shown on the card, move the Barge off the board, remove the Canal Card, and resolve its bottom effects (gaining resources, destroying Townsfolk Cards, or reading Story Book entries). Each Canal section built unlocks new build sites and trades along the river.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Attacking Barbarians
walkthrough.text: To defeat a Barbarian Card, spend the Provisions shown on the board below that Barbarian's space, then spend the Workers shown on the card's left side. Gain the rewards on the card's right side (often Cowrie Shells or Clay), then discard the Barbarian Card. Defeating Barbarians before the Suffer Attacks phase prevents them from damaging your Ready Pile.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Recruiting, Dismissing, and Constructing
walkthrough.text: You can recruit Townsfolk from the card row by paying the Provisions shown below their space (the leftmost is free). Recruited cards go to your Exhausted Pile. You can dismiss Townsfolk by paying the same cost but discarding them for their brown-background items. You can also construct Farms (1 Labourer, 1 Clay, 1 Wood), Outposts (1 Labourer, 1 Clay, 3 Wood), or Huts (1 Labourer, 3 Clay, 1 Wood) on available build sites.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: Using Huts and Trading
walkthrough.text: Place Workers from your supply onto Hut spaces for immediate rewards (similar to Harvest bonuses). Workers placed here return to your supply next Harvest. Trading is available anytime during the game. Your starting trade lets you spend 3 Cowrie Shells for 1 Clay or 1 Wood. More trades unlock as you build Canal sections, including converting Cowrie Shells to Provisions, exhausting cards for shells, and more.

walkthrough.title: Phase 3: Return the Barge
walkthrough.text: If you built a Canal section this round, the Barge is off the board. Place it on the leftmost remaining Canal Card. If you did not build this round, skip this phase. If no Canal Cards remain on the board, there is no need to return the Barge since the game is about to end.

walkthrough.title: Phase 4: Suffer Attacks
walkthrough.text: Each Barbarian Card still above the board now attacks. For each one, you must either pay a bribe (usually a Worker or Resource) or suffer damage by destroying Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile. You choose which option for each Barbarian. This is why defeating Barbarians during the action phase is so important: fewer attackers means less damage to your deck.

walkthrough.title: Phase 5: Refresh the Card Row
walkthrough.text: Slide remaining Townsfolk Cards left to fill gaps. Count how many new Barbarian Cards are needed based on the icons along the top shore of the river. Slide existing Barbarian Cards right to make room, then reveal new Barbarians from right to left. Fill remaining empty spaces with Townsfolk Cards from the Draw Pile. If 7 Barbarian Cards are now above the board, you immediately lose.

walkthrough.title: The Ready Pile and the Flood
walkthrough.text: Your Ready Pile is your lifeline. Drawing and destroying cards depletes it. When it runs empty, shuffle your Exhausted Pile to form a new Ready Pile and advance the Flood one space to the right. If the Flood reaches an unbuilt Canal Card or moves off the board, you lose immediately. Always check how many cards remain in your Ready Pile at the start of each round to estimate how many rounds you have left.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: You win by building all 6 Canal sections and surviving through the end of that round (including Suffer Attacks and Refresh). You lose three ways: the Flood reaches unbuilt Canal, 7 Barbarians accumulate above the board, or you must destroy a Townsfolk Card but have none in your Ready or Exhausted Piles. Upon winning, flip the top Victory Card; upon losing, flip the top Defeat Card. Each directs you to a Story Book entry that advances the campaign.

bullets: 5 phases: Harvest, Take Actions, Return Barge, Suffer Attacks, Refresh Card Row
Repeat until you win or lose

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Round
walkthrough.text: You have the board set up with 6 Canal Cards on the river, a row of Townsfolk and one Barbarian above the board, 10 cards in your Ready Pile, and the Flood at the far left. Let's walk through Round 1 step by step so you understand the flow.

walkthrough.title: Harvest: Draw Your Hand
walkthrough.text: Start by collecting your Harvest. Draw 4 Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile into your hand. Take 1 White Worker (Labourer), 1 Cowrie Shell, and 1 Provision from the Main Supply. You have no constructed Outposts yet, so no bonus shells. That's your entire income for this round.

walkthrough.title: Check Your Ready Pile
walkthrough.text: You started with 10 cards in your Ready Pile and just drew 4. That leaves 6. If your Ready Pile empties, the Flood advances. Keep a mental count at the start of every round. This is the game's internal clock.

walkthrough.title: Review Your Townsfolk Cards
walkthrough.text: Look at the 4 cards in your hand. Each one shows resources on a brown background (gained when played to the Exhausted Pile) and additional items on a red background (gained only when discarding permanently). Decide which cards to play versus discard based on what resources you need this round.

walkthrough.title: Play Some Townsfolk Cards
walkthrough.text: Play 2 or 3 cards from your hand to your Exhausted Pile, collecting the brown-background resources from each. These cards are safe in your Exhausted Pile and will be shuffled back into your Ready Pile eventually. Save at least one card for another purpose.

walkthrough.title: Consider Tucking a Card
walkthrough.text: You have 1 tuck space available beneath the board. If any of your remaining hand cards show good Harvest icons (like a Cowrie Shell or Provision), tuck it there. It will provide that resource every single round for the rest of the game. This is one of the most powerful investments you can make.

walkthrough.title: Check If You Can Build the Canal
walkthrough.text: Look at the first Canal Card. The Barge should be sitting on it. Check the cost printed at the top: it will require specific Workers and Cowrie Shells. In Round 1, you may or may not have enough to build. If you can afford it, great. If not, do not worry; sometimes it takes a round or two to gather resources.

walkthrough.title: Attempt the Canal Build
walkthrough.text: If you have the required Workers and Cowrie Shells, spend them to the Main Supply. Move the Barge off the board, remove the Canal Card, and resolve its bottom effects from left to right. You might gain Clay or Wood, but you will also likely destroy 1 Townsfolk Card from your Ready Pile. That is the cost of progress.

walkthrough.title: Consider Attacking the Barbarian
walkthrough.text: There is 1 Barbarian Card above the board. Check its attack cost: Provisions below on the board plus Workers on the card's left side. Defeating it now means it will not damage you in Phase 4. Check the rewards on the right side too. If you can afford the fight, take it.

walkthrough.title: Recruit a Free Townsfolk
walkthrough.text: The leftmost Townsfolk Card in the card row is always free to recruit. Place it into your Exhausted Pile. This grows your deck for future rounds. Recruiting Townsfolk with useful play or tuck icons is a good early investment.

walkthrough.title: Consider Building a Farm or Outpost
walkthrough.text: Look at your build sites. You start with just 1 available site. Farms cost 1 Labourer, 1 Clay, and 1 Wood, adding Workers or Provisions to future Harvests. Outposts cost 1 Labourer, 1 Clay, and 3 Wood, adding Cowrie Shells and letting you treat Workers as different colors. Build if you have the resources; otherwise, plan for next round.

walkthrough.title: Use Your Remaining Cards
walkthrough.text: Remember: you cannot hold Townsfolk Cards between rounds. If you still have cards in hand, play them to your Exhausted Pile for their brown-background resources, or discard one to gain both brown and red resources if you need something specific. Every card must be used.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Forgetting to Use All Hand Cards
walkthrough.text: A common beginner error is ending the action phase with cards still in hand. You are not allowed to hold cards between rounds. Always play, discard, or tuck every card before moving on. If you forget, just play them to your Exhausted Pile for their basic resources.

walkthrough.title: Phase 3: Return the Barge
walkthrough.text: If you built a Canal section, the Barge is off the board. Place it on the leftmost remaining Canal Card. This marks where the next build will happen. If you did not build, the Barge is still on the board, so skip this step.

walkthrough.title: Phase 4: Suffer Attacks
walkthrough.text: Any Barbarian Cards still above the board now attack. For each one, check the bottom of the card. You can either pay a bribe (often a Worker) or suffer damage by destroying Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile. If you defeated the Barbarian earlier, you skip this entirely for that card.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Ignoring Barbarians
walkthrough.text: New players sometimes focus entirely on building Canals and ignore Barbarians. This is dangerous. Each undefeated Barbarian damages your Ready Pile every round, accelerating the Flood. And during the Refresh phase, new Barbarians arrive. If 7 accumulate, you lose instantly. Balance Canal progress with Barbarian control.

walkthrough.title: Phase 5: Refresh the Card Row
walkthrough.text: Slide remaining Townsfolk Cards to the left. Check the river icons to see how many new Barbarian Cards to add. Add them from the right, then fill empty middle spaces with new Townsfolk Cards. If you now see 7 Barbarian Cards above the board, the game is over.

walkthrough.title: Starting Round 2
walkthrough.text: Begin a new Harvest. Draw cards from your Ready Pile, collect Workers and Resources (including any bonuses from constructed buildings or tucked cards). Notice how your engine is beginning to grow. Each round should feel a little more powerful than the last if you have invested wisely.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake: Never Trading
walkthrough.text: Players often forget the trade row along the river. Your starting trade converts 3 Cowrie Shells into 1 Clay or 1 Wood. As you build Canals, more trades unlock. Trading can be done at any time, not just during the action phase. Use trades to convert surplus resources into what you actually need.

walkthrough.title: Mid-Game Priorities
walkthrough.text: By rounds 3-5, you should be building at least one Canal section per round while keeping Barbarians in check. Prioritize constructing 1-2 buildings early (an Outpost for Cowrie Shell income or a Farm for Workers). Keep tucking high-value Townsfolk Cards. Every addition to your Harvest compounds over the remaining rounds.

walkthrough.title: Approaching the Endgame
walkthrough.text: As you near the final Canal sections, the costs grow steeper and more Barbarians arrive. Count your Ready Pile carefully. If you are close to running out, consider bribing Barbarians instead of letting them destroy cards. Your last round will often require building the Canal and defeating enough Barbarians to stay below 7.

walkthrough.title: Winning or Losing the Game
walkthrough.text: If you build all 6 Canal sections and survive through the Refresh phase without hitting any loss condition, you win. Flip the top Victory Card and read the indicated Story Book entry. If you lose, flip the top Defeat Card and read its entry. Either way, you advance the campaign story and unlock new content for the next game.

walkthrough.title: The Campaign Continues
walkthrough.text: Between games, file cards into their correct box sections. No Workers, Resources, or board state carries over. Shuffle all Townsfolk Cards back together. Reset Canal Cards, Hut Cards, and buildings. The campaign ends after 7 total wins or losses, with the Gold Victory or Defeat Card triggering the final story entry. Each campaign only reveals 40-60 percent of the hidden content, making replays feel fresh.

walkthrough.title: Track Your Ready Pile Religiously
walkthrough.text: At the start of every round, count the cards in your Ready Pile. This is your timer. Divide that number by how many cards you draw each Harvest to estimate how many rounds remain before the Flood advances. Plan your Canal builds and Barbarian fights around this countdown.

walkthrough.title: Tuck Early, Tuck Wisely
walkthrough.text: Tucking a Townsfolk Card beneath the board is like planting a seed. The earlier you tuck, the more rounds it pays off. A card tucked in Round 1 that provides 1 Cowrie Shell will have given you 8-10 shells by game end. Prioritize tucking cards with Cowrie Shells or Provisions since those are always in demand.

walkthrough.title: Build at Least One Canal Per Round
walkthrough.text: You need to build 6 Canals over roughly 8-10 rounds. Skipping a round sets you behind and means more Barbarians accumulate. Treat Canal building as your primary objective each round, then handle Barbarians and buildings with your remaining resources.

walkthrough.title: Do Not Hoard Resources
walkthrough.text: While you can carry Workers and Resources between rounds, there is rarely a good reason to stockpile heavily. Unspent resources represent lost actions. Build something, defeat a Barbarian, or recruit a Townsfolk. Every round you delay an investment, you lose compound value.

walkthrough.title: Outposts Are Incredibly Flexible
walkthrough.text: Outposts let you treat Workers of connected colors interchangeably. This means a Blue Worker can substitute for a Red Worker if those colors are linked by your Outpost. This flexibility often matters more than the Cowrie Shell income. Build an Outpost early to avoid being stuck with the wrong Worker colors.

walkthrough.title: Bribing Is Not Losing
walkthrough.text: During Suffer Attacks, paying a bribe to a Barbarian feels wasteful, but it is often cheaper than losing Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile. A White Worker bribe preserves your deck, slows the Flood timer, and can be regained next Harvest. Think of bribes as insurance.

walkthrough.title: Use Trading to Smooth Resource Gaps
walkthrough.text: The trade row is easy to forget but solves many problems. Need 1 more Clay? Trade 3 Cowrie Shells. Have surplus Provisions? Maybe not, but you can chain trades. Crucially, trades work at any time, even during Suffer Attacks or Refresh. Check the available trades before declaring you cannot afford something.

walkthrough.title: Losing Is Progress
walkthrough.text: Legacy of Yu is a campaign game with a self-balancing system. Losing a game still flips a Defeat Card and advances the story, often unlocking new content and advantages for next time. Do not restart the campaign if you lose early games. The story is designed to flow through both victories and defeats.

bullets: 6 Canals over 8-10 rounds; skipping a round is costly
Treat Canal building as the primary objective

Often more valuable than the Cowrie Shell income

summary.title: Bribing Is Not Losing
bullets: Paying a bribe preserves your Ready Pile and slows the Flood
A White Worker bribe is often cheaper than losing Townsfolk Cards

summary.title: Use Trading to Smooth Resource Gaps
bullets: Trades work at any time, not just during actions
Check trade options before declaring you cannot afford something

summary.title: Losing Is Progress
bullets: Defeat Cards still advance the story and unlock content
The campaign's self-balancing system adapts to your performance

walkthrough.title: Map Your Resource Pipeline Round by Round
walkthrough.text: Before each round, calculate exactly what resources you will gain from Harvest (including Farms, Outposts, tucked cards, and Hut returns) and what your Townsfolk Cards will likely provide. Then compare that against the Canal cost and the Barbarians you need to deal with. Advanced players plan 2-3 rounds ahead, not just the current one.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Outpost Construction Over Farms
walkthrough.text: While Farms provide steady Workers or Provisions, Outposts offer Cowrie Shell income plus the invaluable Worker color flexibility. Cowrie Shells are the most versatile resource because they convert into Clay, Wood, Provisions, or even Workers through the trade row. An early Outpost dramatically improves your adaptability.

walkthrough.title: Optimize Tuck Order for Maximum Compound Value
walkthrough.text: Not all tuck icons are equal. A Cowrie Shell tuck in Round 1 yields roughly 9 shells across a typical game. A Provision tuck is similarly valuable since Provisions fuel both recruiting and Barbarian attacks. Worker tucks are strong if they provide colored Workers you consistently need. Always tuck the highest-impact card first when you unlock a new tuck space.

walkthrough.title: Strategic Discarding for Critical Turns
walkthrough.text: Discarding a Townsfolk Card to the Discard Pile (gaining brown plus red resources) permanently removes it from your deck. This is bad for deck longevity but sometimes essential. On the turn you need to build an expensive Canal section, discarding 1-2 cards for their red-background bonus can provide the exact resources to complete the build. Time these sacrifices for maximum impact.

walkthrough.title: Control Barbarian Timing, Not Just Count
walkthrough.text: Each Canal section you build reveals new Barbarian spawn icons along the river shore. Before building a Canal, check how many new Barbarians will arrive during Refresh. If building would cause 3 new Barbarians this round, consider defeating existing ones first. Sometimes delaying a Canal build by one round is better than facing an unmanageable wave.

walkthrough.title: Manage Deck Thickness Deliberately
walkthrough.text: Your Ready Pile plus Exhausted Pile form your cycling deck. Recruiting Townsfolk increases deck size, which slows Ready Pile depletion (delaying the Flood) but dilutes card quality. Destroying or discarding cards thins your deck, which is efficient but brings the Flood closer. Recruit to buy time; thin when your engine is strong enough to not need extra rounds.

walkthrough.title: Build Huts to Unlock Tuck Spaces and Hut Actions
walkthrough.text: Huts must be built left to right, and each one opens a new Hut space and a new tuck space. The Hut spaces themselves provide repeatable rewards each round (place a Worker, gain an immediate bonus, get the Worker back at Harvest). Combined with the tuck space, each Hut effectively adds two new income sources. Build your first Hut as soon as you can afford the 3 Clay.

walkthrough.title: Use Trades to Chain Resource Conversions
walkthrough.text: As you unlock more trades, chain conversions become possible. For example: exhaust a Townsfolk Card for 2 Cowrie Shells, then use 3 Shells to buy Clay, then later trade Clay for 1 Shell to net other conversions. This lets you turn cards you do not need into exactly the resources you do. The more Canal sections you build, the more flexible your trade network becomes.

walkthrough.title: Plan Your Final Round in Advance
walkthrough.text: The last round is often the most intense. You need to build the final Canal section, survive all Barbarian attacks, and end with fewer than 7 Barbarians after Refresh. Work backward: count the Barbarians that will arrive, the resources you need for the final Canal, and what bribes or attacks you can afford. Stockpile a few extra resources in the penultimate round if needed.

walkthrough.title: Adapt to Campaign Unlocks
walkthrough.text: As you progress through the campaign, new Story Book entries add mechanics, cards, and challenges. Stay flexible in your strategy. A building or card type that was not available in Game 1 might become your strongest tool by Game 4. Re-evaluate your priorities each game based on newly unlocked content rather than rigidly following a single plan.

summary.title: Map Your Resource Pipeline
bullets: Calculate total Harvest income before spending
Plan 2-3 rounds ahead, not just the current round

summary.title: Prioritize Outpost Construction
bullets: Cowrie Shell income + Worker color flexibility
Shells convert to anything via trades

summary.title: Optimize Tuck Order
bullets: Cowrie Shell and Provision tucks compound hardest
Always tuck highest-impact card first when a new space opens

summary.title: Strategic Discarding
bullets: Discard for red-background bonus on critical Canal-build turns
Permanently removes card from deck — use sparingly

summary.title: Control Barbarian Timing
bullets: Check Barbarian spawn icons before building Canal
Sometimes delaying a build avoids an unmanageable wave

summary.title: Manage Deck Thickness
bullets: Recruit to delay Flood; thin when your engine is strong
Balance deck size against card quality

summary.title: Build Huts for Tuck and Hut Spaces
bullets: Each Hut adds a tuck space + a repeatable Hut action
Build first Hut early for compound value

summary.title: Chain Resource Conversions
bullets: Unlock more trades by building Canals
Chain conversions to transform surplus into exact needs

summary.title: Plan Your Final Round
bullets: Work backward: Canal cost + Barbarian attacks + Refresh count
Stockpile a few extra resources the round before

summary.title: Adapt to Campaign Unlocks
bullets: New mechanics arrive via Story Book entries
Re-evaluate priorities each game based on unlocked content

entries.definition: A White Worker. Required for building construction and certain Canal builds. Cannot be substituted by colored Workers unless an Outpost links White to another color.
A Red Worker. Primarily used to attack Barbarians. Can be substituted by other colors if linked through a constructed Outpost.
A Black Worker. Used for attacking certain Barbarians. Can be substituted via Outpost color links.
A Blue Worker. Used for certain Barbarian attacks and building costs. Can be substituted via Outpost color links.
A Yellow Worker. Used for attacking Barbarians. Can be substituted via Outpost color links.
An icon meaning any Worker that is not White (not a Labourer). This includes Red, Black, Blue, or Yellow Workers.
An icon meaning any Worker of any color, including White Labourers.
A currency resource used to build Canal sections and to trade for other resources. Gained from Outposts, Harvest, and defeating Barbarians.
A building material resource used to construct Farms, Outposts, and Huts. Gained from Canal effects, trades, and some Townsfolk Cards.
A building material resource used to construct Farms, Outposts, and Huts. Gained from Canal effects, trades, and some Townsfolk Cards.
A resource used to recruit Townsfolk, attack Barbarians, and in certain trades. Gained from Farms, Harvest, and some Townsfolk Cards.

Your facedown deck of 10 Townsfolk Cards placed below the board during setup. You draw from it during Harvest and destroy from it when taking damage. When it runs out, shuffle your Exhausted Pile to form a new Ready Pile and advance the Flood.
The pile where Townsfolk Cards go after being played from your hand. These cards are safe and will be shuffled into a new Ready Pile when the current one runs out.
The facedown pile of Townsfolk Cards to the left of the board. Cards are drawn from here to fill the card row and for certain effects. Shuffled from discards when empty.
Where permanently discarded Townsfolk Cards go (not the same as the Exhausted Pile). Cards here are shuffled back only when the Townsfolk Draw Pile runs out.
The facedown pile of Barbarian Cards to the right of the board. New Barbarians are revealed from here during the Refresh Card Row phase.
Where defeated Barbarian Cards are placed. Shuffled back into the Barbarian Draw Pile when it runs out.
The row of spaces above the board holding Townsfolk and Barbarian Cards. Townsfolk are on the left, Barbarians on the right. Refreshed at the end of each round.
One of 6 cards placed along the river representing Canal sections to build. Each shows a cost at the top and effects at the bottom. Sorted by jade numbers (1-2, 3-4, 5-6) corresponding to difficulty.
A facedown card placed in the settlement area. When you construct a Hut building, you flip the corresponding Hut Card and resolve its effects. These may trigger Story Book entries.
A multi-use card that can be played for resources (brown background), discarded for extra resources (brown + red), or tucked beneath the board to boost future Harvests.
An enemy card placed in the card row. Shows Worker costs to attack on the left, rewards on the right, and damage or bribe options along the bottom.
A campaign card flipped when you win a game. Directs you to a Story Book entry that advances the campaign narrative and may unlock new content.
A campaign card flipped when you lose a game. Directs you to a Story Book entry based on how you lost (Flood vs. Barbarians/Damage). Still advances the campaign.
A booklet with numbered entries that you read when directed by game effects (Gold Turtle icons). Contains narrative and campaign unlocks. Never read ahead.
A deck of 71 numbered cards containing new Townsfolk, Barbarians, and other content unlocked through Story Book entries. Do not shuffle or browse this deck.
A token on the river that advances 1 space to the right each time your Ready Pile runs out. If it reaches an unbuilt Canal Card or moves off the board, you lose immediately.
A token that sits on the leftmost Canal Card. You can only build the Canal section where the Barge is. After building, the Barge is removed and returned to the next Canal Card during Phase 3.
A square space below the river where Farms, Outposts, or Huts can be constructed. You start with 1 and unlock more as you build Canal sections.
A space on the board where you can place a Worker for an immediate reward. The Worker returns to your supply during the next Harvest. More spaces are unlocked by constructing Huts.
A space beneath the board where a Townsfolk Card can be permanently placed to add its harvest icons to every future round. You start with 1 and gain more by building Huts.
A building costing 1 Labourer, 1 Clay, and 1 Wood. Once constructed on a build site, it adds Workers or Provisions to your Harvest each round.
A building costing 1 Labourer, 1 Clay, and 3 Wood. Adds Cowrie Shell income to your Harvest and lets you treat connected Worker colors as interchangeable.
A building costing 1 Labourer, 3 Clay, and 1 Wood. Must be built in order left to right. Unlocks a Hut space and a tuck space. Flipping its Hut Card may trigger story effects.
Phase 1 of each round. You draw Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile, gain Workers and Resources from the Harvest row, and return Workers from Hut spaces to your supply.
Phase 2 of each round. You play Townsfolk Cards, build Canals, attack Barbarians, recruit or dismiss Townsfolk, construct buildings, use Huts, and trade. Continues until you choose to stop.
Phase 3 of each round. If you built a Canal section, return the Barge to the leftmost remaining Canal Card. Skipped if no Canal was built.
Phase 4 of each round. Each remaining Barbarian above the board forces you to either pay a bribe or destroy Townsfolk Cards from your Ready Pile.
Phase 5 of each round. Slide Townsfolk left, add new Barbarians from the right based on river shore icons, fill remaining gaps with Townsfolk. 7 Barbarians = instant loss.
